commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Lack of visual separation or containers for the 5-day forecast columns makes the layout feel slightly cluttered.
issues.fix: Add a subtle background or border-radius to each individual forecast day block to distinguish them from the main card body.

issues.description: The forecast items are static; adding hover effects would improve user engagement.
issues.fix: Add a transition or subtle background shift on hover for the individual forecast stack items to indicate they are interactive elements.

issues.description: The logic for the current weather icon is missing, despite being a requirement in the prompt.
issues.fix: Add a dynamic mapping function to render an icon/emoji based on the 'condition' prop rather than assuming a static display.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and uses the design system primitives effectively. The layout is clean and logical, though it would benefit from more visual distinction in the forecast section and dynamic icon mapping based on the condition prop.

evaluation.critique: The component has good structure and hierarchy with proper use of ggui primitives, but falls short on design tokens consistency (some fallback hex colors, missing spacing tokens) and lacks interactive polish (no hover states, transitions, or weather-condition emoji mapping). Data presentation is mostly solid but relies on prop consumers to provide properly formatted values.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

issues.description: The requirements stated: 'Final review step showing all answers before submission' and 'Textarea for open-ended comments'. The current implementation puts the comment field inside the review step, making it editable during review, rather than a distinct step/field prior to the final summary.
issues.fix: Move the Comment textarea to its own dedicated step (Step 4) and make the final review (Step 5) a read-only confirmation screen.

issues.description: Lack of ARIA landmarks and fieldset wrapping for radio/checkbox groups.
issues.fix: Wrap radio and checkbox groups in semantic fieldset elements or ensure the design system components provide correct ARIA labelling for group inputs.

issues.description: The error state management updates immediately, showing an 'Name is required' error even when the user hasn't interacted with the field yet (pristine state).
issues.fix: Implement 'touched' or 'blurred' state tracking to only show error messages after the user has interacted with the input.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, functional, and well-structured. The primary issue is the workflow logic where the open-ended comments were bundled into the summary step rather than preceding it, and minor UX improvements regarding validation feedback timing would elevate it to production-ready.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid structure and good data handling, but uses some hardcoded values (padding={24}, gap={24}, gap={16}) instead of design tokens, and the step 3 validation requires at least one feature which may be too restrictive for some use cases. Overall well-executed multi-step form with proper validation and state management.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

issues.description: Task title editing functionality mentioned in the prompt is missing.
issues.fix: Add an edit mode (e.g., toggle state or inline input) for the task title when double-clicked or via an edit button.

issues.description: The Move To dropdown inside the card re-renders the whole board on change without a loading state.
issues.fix: Implement a subtle loading indicator or transition feedback when a task is moved to signal the update.

issues.description: Lack of ARIA roles for the Kanban board structure; interactive elements in cards (Select, Add Task) could benefit from better focus management.
issues.fix: Use aria-labels on the task cards and ensure the Add Task flow manages keyboard focus appropriately.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and follows design system guidelines effectively. It covers the core requirements well, though it misses the specific request for inline task title editing. Adding focus management and some minor UI polish for transitions would elevate this to production-ready.

evaluation.critique: The component correctly implements a 3-column Kanban layout with proper use of design tokens for spacing and colors. However, it lacks hover/focus states on interactive elements, has some minor UX issues (e.g., title not editable on double-click as specified), and the move dropdown on every card creates visual clutter—consider using drag-and-drop or a more compact interaction pattern.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The legend for categories, explicitly requested in the prompt, is missing from the implementation.
issues.fix: Add a Legend component mapping the CATEGORY_COLORS object to visual indicators below the grid.

issues.description: The interactive grid cells lack visual feedback on hover.
issues.fix: Add a :hover style to the grid items (e.g., scale slightly or darken the background) to improve user affordance.

issues.description: The grid cells are missing keyboard event handling for the 'Enter' or 'Space' keys, despite being interactive.
issues.fix: Add an onKeyDown handler to trigger setSelected when 'Enter' or ' ' is pressed on a focused cell.

evaluations.critique: The implementation correctly utilizes CSS Grid to replicate the periodic table layout and manages state effectively. It is a solid foundation, but the omission of the requested legend and the lack of keyboard/hover feedback limits the overall professional polish.

evaluation.critique: The component has strong design token usage and clear hierarchy, but lacks visual polish with minimal hover/focus states and missing keyboard navigation (arrow keys). The grid layout requires a 900px minimum width which may cause responsiveness issues on smaller screens, and the legend mentioned in the prompt is not implemented.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

example.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

issues.description: The timestamp prop is present in the interface but is not displayed in the message list as requested.
issues.fix: Add a Text component showing the formatted timestamp below the message text in the list.

issues.description: The component uses a useEffect hook to mirror props.messages into local state, which can lead to synchronization issues if props update frequently.
issues.fix: Derive message state directly from props or use a more robust synchronization strategy if local updates are necessary.

issues.description: The scroll container is nested inside another Box, which makes the layout slightly redundant and potentially causes alignment issues with the scrollbar.
issues.fix: Apply the scroll overflow properties directly to the parent Box acting as the message list container.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and handles the grouping logic and interaction states effectively. It misses the specific requirement to display timestamps, but otherwise provides a solid, functional chat interface.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid layout structure with proper flex usage and scrolling behavior, but mixes design tokens inconsistently—using var(--ggui-color-*) correctly for colors while hardcoding pixel values (16, 12, 8) for spacing instead of var(--ggui-spacing-*) tokens. Message grouping, timestamp formatting, and data handling are well-implemented, though timestamp display is missing from the UI despite being available in the data.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

issues.description: The logic for the grid layout (3 per row) is handled by 'auto-fit', which is flexible but might not strictly adhere to the 3-per-row requirement on specific desktop widths.
issues.fix: Use explicit grid template columns like 'repeat(3, 1fr)' with a media query or a container constraint to ensure 3 per row on desktop.

issues.description: There is no visual feedback or transition when the data updates via the custom event.
issues.fix: Add a simple CSS transition or flash effect on the price text when data updates to signal change to the user.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, professional, and makes excellent use of the design system primitives. It successfully implements the requested color-coded logic and layout requirements, though fine-tuning the grid to be strictly 3 columns on desktop would improve layout predictability.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid responsive layout and data handling, but relies on fallback hardcoded colors for semantic tokens (onSurfaceVariant, success, error) rather than pure token usage. The polish is good overall with proper icon usage and aria-live regions, though hover/focus states are missing from interactive elements and the loading state could be enhanced.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: Lack of visual transition effects between steps.
issues.fix: Wrap the step content in a motion component or simple CSS transition to avoid an abrupt jump when switching steps.

issues.description: Input validation error state is not communicated to the user.
issues.fix: Add a validation message area below the inputs that appears when the email is invalid so users know why the 'Next' button remains disabled.

issues.description: The layout is somewhat rigid; could benefit from more consistent spacing between the Review summary and the final action button.
issues.fix: Add a more explicit margin or spacer between the review box and the action buttons.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, follows all logic requirements, and correctly handles state. The UI is clean, though it lacks subtle transition animations and explicit error feedback for the required fields, which would elevate the user experience.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid layout structure and good hierarchy with clear step progression, but design token usage is inconsistent—some spacing uses hardcoded values (16, 8, 24px) instead of var(--ggui-spacing-*), and background colors mix tokens with fallback hex values. Polish is adequate with validation and disabled states, but lacks transition animations and visual feedback on form errors.
